description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a striking blue and gold color scheme, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts. The fuselage is slender and painted blue, with a prominent vertical stabilizer at the tail, adorned with a cross insignia. The cockpit is open and situated near the front of the fuselage, just behind the engine and propeller assembly. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a smaller tail wheel, all fixed and exposed. The wings are detailed with gold striping and rivet accents, giving the aircraft a classic, early 20th-century appearance. The overall design suggests a historical or replica aircraft, likely inspired by World War I-era biplanes.
description: A blue and gold vintage biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear with the propeller assembly visible.
description_view_1: Side angle highlighting the length of the fuselage, the cross insignia on the tail, and the layered wing structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, vertical stabilizer, and the full span of the biplane's wings.
description_view_3: Oblique rear-side view emphasizing the tail design, wing struts, and the open cockpit's position relative to the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the engine, likely single-seat with no visible canopy.
